name: Nigeria
avoid: April to October is wetter across most of this route, so road transfers get slower and outdoor days in Lagos and the southwest feel stickier.
Domestic flight schedules can shift at short notice; keep a buffer before long-haul departures and reconfirm Air Peace or Dana Air sectors the night before.
Card payments are improving in major cities, but intercity buses, market stops, and smaller attraction add-ons still work more smoothly with some naira cash.
stopoverNote: Lagos is the easiest international gateway and the best place to start or finish; use road transfers for Abeokuta and Osogbo, then save time with domestic hops on the Abuja and Calabar legs instead of trying to force everything overland.

cities.name: Lagos
cities.notes: Nike Art Gallery, Lekki Conservation Centre, BRT buses, and the country's busiest food-and-nightlife corridor around Victoria Island and Lekki

cities.name: Abuja
cities.notes: Aso Rock viewpoints, Millennium Park, Jabi Lake, museums, and the easiest domestic-flight hub for moving between the southwest and Cross River

cities.name: Calabar
cities.notes: Marina Resort, Tinapa, Duke Town history, and straightforward day-trip access toward Kwa Falls and other Cross River nature stops

cities.name: Abeokuta
cities.notes: Olumo Rock, adire textile heritage, Kuti family history, and an easy first overland stop out of Lagos

cities.name: Osogbo
cities.notes: Osun-Osogbo Sacred Grove, Susanne Wenger sculpture trails, and a slower cultural stop between southwest road legs

itinerary.sim: MTN Nigeria and Airtel are the easiest picks for this route; budget roughly ₹530–₹1,590 for a starter data bundle with enough coverage for maps, ride-hailing, and messaging across Lagos, Abuja, and Calabar, with patchier speeds on longer road stretches.

Tinapa resort-area browse and lakeside time
Slave History Museum or local heritage stop
Return transfer planning for Lagos or onward travel

connections.method: Intercity buses and shared cars via the Lagos-Abeokuta Expressway are the easy budget move; leave early to avoid worst commuter traffic out of Lagos
Bus or hired car via Ibadan keeps the southwest loop compact and is easier than backtracking to Lagos first
Overnight or daytime intercity buses are the value option; if time matters more, road back toward Lagos or Ibadan for a domestic flight onward
connections.cost: ₹850–₹3,180 by bus; ₹5,300–₹13,250 with a flight sector

connections.method: Air Peace or Dana Air is the practical way to keep this route efficient; going all-road turns it into a full travel day
Domestic flight back to Lagos is the clean finish for onward international departures and saves a long return overland haul

extras: November to March is the cleanest window for this loop: Lagos is less waterlogged, southwest road journeys are easier, and Calabar's outdoor days are more manageable than in the wetter months.
Use BRT selectively in Lagos for predictable trunk routes, but keep Uber or Bolt handy for first-mile and last-mile hops on the Island and for early airport runs.
Abeokuta and Osogbo work best as short overland stops from Lagos rather than stand-alone flight destinations, so pack them close together before moving north or east.

If you want wildlife rather than only city-and-culture time, the easiest place to bolt on Yankari is around Abuja, but it makes most sense as an overnight or two-day detour rather than a same-day dash.
Cross River days often run best with a hired car or driver once you leave central Calabar, especially if you want Kwa Falls or Drill Ranch without losing time to ad-hoc transport searches.
